June was pretty busy for people farming rewards↓

1️⃣ @TrueCurrentX is the best active for traders right now

They have 0% trading fees, RFQ execution, self-custody, iOS mobile support, and access to more than just crypto majors in one seamless venue.

Your trading activity earns Airdrop Points, and users unlock their own referral code after $10k in cumulative notional volume.

After that, referrers earn a 20% bonus on the Points generated by referred traders, while referred users still keep their full Points.

So honestly, this makes sense if you're already trading perps, because that same activity can build points and give you exposure to a new venue early.

2️⃣ @grvt_io is running Season 2.0 rewards

Trading volume gets the biggest share of weekly points, but there is also things like:

→ deposits
→ strategies
→ referrals

They also have the 250K Volume Challenge running in June, with the prize pool starting at 50,000 USDT and scaling up to 250,000 USDT.

3️⃣ @paradex is running Season 3 XP, with 4M XP distributed weekly

Trading, liquidity, and community activity generate XP, while referrals add another layer on top.

Referrers earn 10% of referees’ XP, and referred users get a 5% XP bonus on trading activity.

It’s not the newest opportunity, but the weekly distribution makes it easier to track than campaigns where nobody knows what the points even mean.

4️⃣ @Polymarket’s current reward angle is more about liquidity rewards

For the World Cup 2026 markets, makers can earn by posting resting limit orders, with rewards distributed daily.

This isn’t for everyone because market making is more involved than just placing a trade. But it’s a good example of where reward campaigns are going.

My take:

I genuinely think that reward farming is getting more serious because the best opportunities right now are the ones where normal product usage creates reward exposure.

Obviously, none of this is free money, but if you’re already active in these markets anyway, it makes sense to know which venues are rewarding the activity you were probably going to do regardless.

tweets.text:
Raw volume can often be a vanity metric.

It's easily manipulated and inflated by points farming, fee promos, and routing tricks.

Here's how the top wallets rank on 90d revenue across swaps and perps:

→ @BinanceWallet: $136.8M (estimated)
→ @phantom: $23.3M (measured)
→ @MetaMask: $7.9M (measured + fee-derived)
→ @TrustWallet: $3.5M (fee-derived)
→ @rabby_io: $1.9M (measured)
→ @Rainbowdotme: $190K (fee-derived)

Measured/Fee-derived = there are onchain, verifiable fees.
Estimated (in this context) = no fees, so the number is hypothetical.

The 90 revenue number for Binance has 2 important caveats:

→ 0 % fee promo
→ Concentrated audience (57% of volume comes from the top 1,000 addresses farming Alpha points)

So that revenue number is what they'd hypothetically earn if they were charging fees, on volume that's mostly farming. It's the same vanity problem, just one layer up.

I could've easily made a "Binance Wallet is the leader in wallet revenue", but IMO, context is even more important than raw numbers.

Data from @ax1vc.

> I know I’m not a simulated algorithm. The simulated algorithm isn’t conscious (we can stipulate). I am.

My interpretation of FDT has always been that you're supposed to conceive of your conscious self as simultaneously operating on all instantiations of it - on both your physical arm picking up the box, and the virtual arm inside of the simulation. Your consciousness is not situated either in the physical you alone, or in the simulated you alone. It is situated in the entire equivalence class of instantiations that are running the algorithm that is "you".

thread_tweets.text: And in terms of real world applicability, clearly the capability to do that level of faithful simulation does not exist, but on the other hand, our algorithms are leaky - lots of people can tell intuitively when someone is being dishonest. The most important core insight is that the conscious actions of our physical body are far from the only channel through which our mind's internal strategizing acts on the real world, and as a direct result of this, many forms of "machiavellian" strategizing often end up greatly underperforming what simple game theory models would predict. For these kinds of reasons folk morality is already somewhere in between CDT and FDT, even if not consciously stated.

The World Cup games are really something else. Beyond the competition and the games themselves—which are fantastic—there’s this incredible element of how people from different countries interact with each other.
People bring their unique personalities and nationalities, and everyone just gets along with so much respect. Whether it’s the Scots bringing their spirit and bagpipes to the games, or the Japanese fans cleaning up the stadium after a match, it’s a beautiful reflection of what the United Nations—and all countries—should be.
It brings out a joy, laughter, and a warmth that gives us a real element of optimism. We should learn from this whole experience of what it's like to be together, and how we can be together.
#WorldCup #soccer #raydalio

tweets.text:
Gavin Baker: “I think Anthropic is worth $3 trillion today.”

Gavin laid out the case on the most recent All-In Podcast:

– They will end 2026 with over $100B in revenue

– They will likely end 2028 with $200-$300B in revenue

– At that scale, Anthropic will be very profitable because compute will be inference-dominated

– Anthropic reportedly has 85% gross margins on inference

– The market has already absorbed Anthropic’s valuation, it will just shift from private to public

@Jason:

“I'm sorry, did you say Anthropic is worth $3 trillion?”

Gavin:

“Yeah, I think that is roughly where it would probably trade as a public company.”

Travis Kalanick:

“Wow. Oh man. Holy sh*t.”

Gavin:

“They're going to end this year with well over $100 billion (in revenue). So what's the '28 number? Is it $200 billion? Is it $300 billion?”

“It's probably not going to trade at 10 times that number, and it will be very profitable at that scale because it'll be inference-dominated and people are reporting they have 85% gross margins on inference.”

“But in terms of the market absorbing this, like the market's already absorbed it. It's just shifting from private to public.”

“And so in the scale of global capital markets, these seem like really big numbers, but you're just moving from the private markets to the public markets, which are even bigger.”
